title: Enter the Transformer
concept: Discarding recurrence enabled training models on the entire web, launching the modern LLM era. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Transformer Foundations.'

exercises.prompt: How did AI models read text before the Transformer was invented?

options.text: All words at the exact same time

exercises.explain: Old AI models read text very slowly, word-by-word. The Transformer changed everything by reading whole sentences all at once.

cards.title: The Great AI Leap
cards.body: Before 2017, AI read books word-by-word, which took forever. Then came the Transformer—a breakthrough that allowed computers to read millions of pages at the same time, giving birth to ChatGPT!

cards.title: What is a Transformer?
cards.body: The Transformer is a special AI structure that processes all words in a sentence at once. By ditching the slow, step-by-step reading of older models, it unlocked the ability to learn from the entire web.

cards.title: The Bird's-Eye View
cards.body: Think of reading a book. Older AI models read word-by-word like a slow caterpillar crawling along a line of text. The Transformer is like a bird soaring high above the page, looking at all the sentences at the same exact time.

cards.title: Training at Warp Speed
cards.body: Imagine training an AI on all of Wikipedia. An old model would take years, reading word-by-word. A Transformer splits the text into thousands of chunks and reads them simultaneously, finishing the training in just days.

cards.title: It's Not Just Big Computers
cards.body: Many people think AI got smarter just because computers got bigger. But without the Transformer's design, even the biggest supercomputers would still be stuck slowly reading sentences word-by-word!

cards.title: Slow vs. Instant Reading
cards.body: Compare how old AI models read compared to the modern Transformer model.

left.text: Read text word... by... word, which is slow and hard to scale.

right.text: Reads all words at once, scaling up to learn from the whole web.

exercises.prompt: Why did the Transformer architecture make it possible to train models on the entire web?

options.text: It processes all words at once instead of one-by-one
It uses secret human writers to do the work
It only reads the first letter of each page
It requires turning off the internet during training

exercises.explain: Because Transformers process all words at the same time, they can learn from massive amounts of data, like the entire web, very quickly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Getting rid of word-by-word reading is what made modern Large Language Models (LLMs) possible.
exercises.explain: Discarding recurrence (step-by-step reading) allowed AI to train on massive amounts of web data, creating the modern LLMs we use today.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to show what AI engineers removed to speed up training.
exercises.sentence: To train on the entire web, scientists had to discard slow step-by-step recurrence.
exercises.explain: Ditching step-by-step recurrence let models look at everything at once, allowing them to train on massive datasets like the entire web.

exercises.prompt: Identify the modern Transformer-based AI system.

options.text: ChatBot-X: Analyzes all words in a sentence simultaneously to instantly write a story.
ChatBot-Y: Must wait to read word #1, then word #2, then word #3 before it can react.
ChatBot-Z: Translates sentences by looking up each individual word in a physical dictionary.

exercises.explain: ChatBot-X uses the Transformer approach by looking at the whole sentence at once, making it the AI model. ChatBot-Y reads word-by-word, which is the outdated method.

exercises.prompt: How did discarding recurrence (word-by-word reading) launch the modern LLM era?

options.text: By forcing models to read slower and more carefully
By allowing computers to process all words in a text at the same time
By deleting the internet so models had less to learn
By requiring human tutors to read every sentence out loud

exercises.explain: By looking at the entire web at once instead of word-by-word, Transformers cracked the code to modern AI! Next up: Transformer Foundations.
